Course Title: AI and Regulatory Compliance in Financial Services
Category: Finance & Professional Services: Banking, Insurance, Accounting, Consulting, Legal, Real Estate
Who is this for: Compliance Officers, Risk Managers, Financial Analysts, Bank Managers, Insurance Underwriters, Accountants, Finance Directors, Legal Counsel, RegTech Specialists, Investment Advisors
Team or Dept this is for: Compliance, Risk Management, Legal, Finance, Operations, IT, Internal Audit
Description: Why AI increases scrutiny from regulators.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with financial services (such as banking, insurance, or accounting) and a general awareness of what artificial intelligence means in a business context are all that is needed to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key reasons why AI adoption in financial services attracts increased regulatory scrutiny and explain the core compliance obligations that firms must understand when deploying AI-driven tools. You will also be able to apply foundational frameworks to recognise potential regulatory risks within banking, insurance, accounting, and related professional services contexts.
